name: Pico del Nevero y Alto de Porrinoso — circular desde el Puerto de Navafría: Parque Natural Sierra Norte de Guadarrama
region_intro: Hiking around Aldealengua De Pedraza offers access to diverse landscapes within the Segovia province, including a significant portion of the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park. The terrain features pine forests, extensive scrubland, and meadows, particularly around the Pinar de Navafría. The Ceguilla River and various streams traverse the area, contributing to the natural environment. Hikers can explore mountain peaks like Pico del Nevero and viewpoints such as Mirador del Raso Matacán, providing panoramic vistas. Best hiking trails around Aldealengua De Pedraza The most popular hiking route is Pico del Nevero y Alto de Porrinoso — circular desde el Puerto de Navafría: Parque Natural Sierra Norte de Guadarrama, a 7.2 miles (11.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 48 minutes to complete. This moderate route ascends to Pico del Nevero within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pico del Nevero and Alto del Porrinoso Loop, a moderate 6.8 miles (11.0 km) path. This circular route offers views of the surrounding mountain landscape and pine forests. Local hikers also love the Mirador Cerro Las Coronas y Presa Ceguilla — circular por el Parque Nacional Sierra de Guadarrama, a 13.4 miles (21.6 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, often completed in about 6 hours 35 minutes. Hiking around Aldealengua De Pedraza is defined by high mountain pine forests, river valleys, and peaks offering extensive views, alongside historical shepherd's huts. The network offers options for easy walks and more challenging ascents. The routes in Aldealengua De Pedraza are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 170 reviews. More than 1,400 hikers have used komoot to explore Aldealengua De Pedraza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Aldealengua De Pedraza?
A: There are over 90 hiking trails around Aldealengua De Pedraza, offering a wide range of options from easy walks to more challenging ascents within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park and surrounding areas.

Q: Are there any easy hiking routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Aldealengua De Pedraza offers 14 easy hiking routes. These trails are generally shorter and have less elevation gain, making them ideal for beginners or families looking for a relaxed outdoor experience. While specific easy routes are not listed here, you can find many options on komoot.

Q: What kind of natural landmarks can I expect to see on the trails?
A: The trails around Aldealengua De Pedraza offer diverse natural landmarks. You can ascend to prominent peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4078211" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nevero Peak&lt;/a&gt;, explore mountain passes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2347605" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Linera&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4003184" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navafría Pass&lt;/a&gt;, and discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3028592" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrera de San Mamés&lt;/a&gt; waterfall. The region is also characterized by extensive pine forests, meadows, and the Ceguilla River.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, several popular routes are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1154708610/pico-del-nevero-y-alto-de-porrinoso-circular-desde-el-puerto-de-navafria-parque-natural-sierra-norte-de-guadarrama" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Nevero y Alto de Porrinoso — circular desde el Puerto de Navafría&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e957845513/pico-del-nevero-and-alto-del-porrinoso-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Nevero and Alto del Porrinoso Loop&lt;/a&gt; are excellent circular options that take you through the high mountain landscape.

Q: What are the best viewpoints for panoramic views?
A: For spectacular panoramic views, consider trails that lead to Pico del Nevero (2,209 m), which offers expansive vistas of the Lozoya Valley and Peñalara massif. The Mirador del Raso Matacán (1500 m) also provides excellent views of the surrounding geographical features, including the Puerto de Linera and the Ceguilla river ravine.

Q: Can I hike with my dog in Aldealengua De Pedraza?
A: Many trails in the Aldealengua De Pedraza area are dog-friendly, especially those outside the most sensitive areas of the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park. However, it's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife, and to check specific park regulations if entering designated national park zones.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in this region?
A: The best time to hike in Aldealengua De Pedraza generally depends on your preference. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes, with wildflowers in spring and rich colors in autumn. Summer can be warm, especially at lower elevations, but higher mountain trails provide cooler conditions. Winter hiking is possible, particularly in the pine forests, but requires appropriate gear for snow and ice.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions along the hiking routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. You can discover traditional shepherd's huts (Chozos de Pastores) scattered throughout the landscape, which are unique ethnographic attractions. The ancient Cañada Real Soriana Occidental, a transhumance route, also passes through the municipality. Additionally, the 12th-13th century Romanesque Church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción, known as 'The cathedral of the sierra,' is situated in a beautiful ash forest.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads?
A: Many popular trailheads, such as those near Puerto de Navafría, offer designated parking areas. It's common for routes starting from mountain passes or specific recreational zones to have facilities for vehicles. Always check the starting point of your chosen route for specific parking information.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Aldealengua De Pedraza?
A: The trails in Aldealengua De Pedraza are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 170 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from high mountain pine forests to river valleys, and the extensive views offered from peaks and viewpoints. The mix of natural beauty and historical elements like shepherd's huts also contributes to a memorable experience.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to a waterfall?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3028592" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrera de San Mamés&lt;/a&gt; is a notable waterfall in the area that can be reached via hiking trails. It's a popular natural highlight for those looking to experience the region's water features.

Q: Can I combine hiking with a visit to a nearby village?
A: Absolutely. The medieval walled village of Pedraza is just a few minutes away from Aldealengua De Pedraza. It offers a charming detour with its historic streets and castle, allowing you to combine your nature activities with cultural exploration. The 12th-century Romanesque Ermita de la Virgen de las Vegas is also nearby.
tips: The lagoons of the Hoyos de Pinilla are a jewel of the Sierra de Guadarrama, they do not have the greatness and magnitude of the cirques of the Peñalara massif but they are living and vital remains of the glacial events that modeled these mountains. The water level depends on the season and rainfall, in winter they are usually frozen and in summer there are barely two pools of water. The best time is spring, during the thaw.

The trail leaves the pine forest and continues up the mountain range to the cliffs of Alto del Puerto. The hard slopes are over. The path markings continue through the rope meadows towards the main summit of the massif. They first pass through the valley formed by the pass where the Hornillo stream is born and continue to the block at the geodesic vertex of Nevero (2,209 m). In the surroundings of the summit there are remains of old constructions and old stone walls. The views are spectacular. On a clear, cloudless day, the Lozoya Valley is beautiful, surrounded by the reliefs of Cuerda Larga, the Altos de Hontanares and forests on all the slopes. The chimneys of the towns always release some smoke to enrich the mountain landscape with rural life. And at the head of the valley, the Peñalara massif stands out on the isolated and energetic peak line, it is the great sentinel of the Sierra de Guadarrama.

In the Alto de Porrinoso there are remains of a shepherd's corral or some military post from the Civil War. In the Montes Carpetanos there are quite a few remains of military posts from a front that was active during the years of the Civil War. The walk to the Porrinoso cliffs offers a close view of the Hoyos and Hoyo Cerrado glacial cirques, which are impressive and show traces of the glacial apparatus that covered the mountain range in other times.

The Navafría pass is located in a beautiful mountain pass in the Montes Carpetanos between the Lozoya Valley and the cattle-raising villages of Alto Cega, on the Segovian plateau, in the Cañada Real Soriana Occidental pass. In the port car park there is space for ten or twelve vehicles and when it fills up you have to return about 500 meters along the road to Lozoya to leave the car in the Las Lagunillas car park. In winter this car park can have access problems due to ice and snow. The mountain route leaves the pass heading west to the right of a wooden fence. A few meters to the right begins a forest track that passes through the Navalcollado viewpoint and heads towards the forests and streams where the river Cega is born.
